## [00:00 - 01:12]

This one skill set will add a whole new dimension to your communication skills. What am I talking about? Hand gestures. You see, just like with your vocabulary when you speak, you can improve and increase your vocabulary. You can do the same thing with your gesture vocabulary. When you learn how to use your hand gestures, your gestures support the things that you say. Notice how that's better than the powerful thing about hand gestures is that it supports the things that you say. The easiest way to expand your gesture vocabulary is to think to yourself, how can I use my hands to describe the things that I'm saying? For example, last night I went to dinner and I walked up to a door and then I grabbed the door and I kept pushing it and I couldn't open the door some reason. I couldn't understand why I couldn't push the door. And then I realized, ah, it's not a push door, it's one of those pull doors. Notice how I use my hands to bring that moment to life. A lot of my students say, Vin, I talk about things that are really boring. How can I bring it to life? You can bring it to life with your body language, with your hand gestures. And that door thing actually happened. Craig, can we roll it? You're going to get some tight food and it's pull, not push. Classing, classical. Unication is what I do.
